sentence: Subjective:
The patient, Alexis, presents with a 6-month history of intermittent numbness and tingling in her right hand, which has become more persistent over the last month.
She reports some pain, primarily when typing on the keyboard, and mentions that ibuprofen and rest help alleviate the symptoms.
The numbness and tingling are present all the time, and she experiences pain when moving her mouse.
She denies any pain or numbness at night, and her symptoms are not affecting her sleep.
She uses ibuprofen for pain management.
She reports numbness in her thumb, index finger, and middle finger, with the other two fingers feeling normal.
She has less grip strength on the right hand, and a positive Tinel's sign is present.
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam:
The patient has pain on extension and flexion of the wrist.
She has numbness in the thumb, index finger, and middle finger, with the other two fingers feeling normal.
She has a positive Tinel's sign.
Grip strength is less on the right hand.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has carpal tunnel syndrome, likely due to repetitive strain from data entry.
Problem list: Carpal tunnel syndrome; active; suspected nerve compression in the wrist.
Plan:
I prescribed ibuprofen 600 mg four times a day for pain management.
I ordered an EMG and nerve conduction study to confirm the diagnosis.
I will have the patient wear a wrist splint at night to help alleviate symptoms.
If the test results are positive, we will discuss further treatment options, including physical therapy or surgery.
Follow-up:
I will schedule a follow-up appointment to review the EMG and nerve conduction study results.
